name: St. Colman & Affiliates
description_short: Federal credit union chartered in 1939 offering member-owned financial services including savings accounts, loans, and online banking to Cleveland-area residents.
description_long:
St. Colman & Affiliates Federal Credit Union was established in 1939 as a federally chartered credit union serving the Cleveland, Ohio community. As a member-owned, not-for-profit institution, it operates under the regulatory oversight of the National Credit Union Administration (NCUA), an independent agency of the U.S. Government. The credit union is rooted in the cooperative movement's democratic principles, emphasizing member benefit over corporate profit.

The organization provides a range of financial services designed for personal member benefit, including share savings accounts, share certificates, loan products, and online banking access. Members can apply for loans through downloadable application forms and access their accounts through the credit union's online banking platform. The institution maintains a physical branch location at 6637 Lorain Ave. in Cleveland and offers 24-hour automated services through their "Skippy Line" phone system.

St. Colman distinguishes itself through its long operational history and mission-driven focus on helping members achieve personal financial success through low-cost services. As a federally chartered credit union, member savings are protected by NCUA insurance up to $250,000, providing security comparable to bank deposits. The organization actively promotes accessibility features on its website and maintains direct communication channels via phone, email, and fax.

The credit union serves a niche market of members in the Cleveland area seeking member-owned financial alternatives. However, the website provides limited detailed information about specific loan products, interest rates, membership requirements, or competitive advantages. Prospective members would need to contact the institution directly to understand full service offerings and eligibility criteria.
services: Online banking platform with account access and new account enrollment
pros: NCUA-insured savings up to $250,000 provides strong deposit protection
Federally chartered since 1939 demonstrates long-established stability and operational history
Member-owned, not-for-profit structure returns benefits to members rather than shareholders
Online banking platform available with new account sign-up capability
24-hour automated "Skippy Line" phone system for member access
Accessible website design with contrast, font, and readability features
Multiple contact methods available: phone (216-281-4570), fax, and email
cons: Website lacks transparency on specific loan types, rates, terms, and membership eligibility requirements
Limited service information makes it difficult to compare offerings with other credit unions
Physical branch appears to be single location in Cleveland, limiting accessibility for broader geographic membership
No published details on loan application approval timelines or processing
Website does not clearly explain membership requirements or who qualifies to join
best_for: Cleveland-area residents seeking member-owned financial institution with NCUA protection
Members valuing long-established credit unions with cooperative movement principles
Individuals looking for low-cost loans and savings products within a local community
diagnosis: St. Colman & Affiliates FCU is best for Cleveland-area residents who prioritize member-owned financial institutions and value long-term stability, but prospective members should contact the credit union directly to understand specific loan products, rates, and membership eligibility since the website provides limited service details.
typical_results_timeline: Membership approval and account opening typically takes 1-3 business days. Loan decisions are usually faster than traditional banks.
meta_description: St. Colman & Affiliates — NCUA-insured credit union in Cleveland, OH. Member-owned, not-for-profit. Loans, savings, and member services.
